messages.content: It appears that you may have forgotten to include the text you want to be translated into English. Please provide the text you'd like to have translated, and I'll do my best to help you.

바꿔 말해 호영의 신고식이 순조롭게 치러지고 있다는 뜻이었다.

호영은 그날 훈련을 통해 많은 것을 얻어냈다.

훈련시간도 짧고 선수간의 교류가 적다보니 첫날부터 많은 것을 얻어낼 순 없었지만, 카를로스와 호빙요의 U급 재능을 가져오는 데 50% 정도 성공하였다.

훗날 그들의 S급 재능을 탐할 수 있는 발판을 마련한 셈이었다.

그것도 단 하루 만에 말이다.

‘좋았어.’

모든 것은 순차적으로 진행되고 있었다.

또한 앞으로의 계획을 세우는 데 오늘 훈련이 큰 도움이 되었다.

‘일단 놓치기에 가장 아까운 선수부터 탐하자.’

올 시즌을 끝으로 은퇴할 예정인 지단의 볼 트래핑 재능이 가장 시급했다.

히든조건은 ‘1부 리그로 승격하기’로, 간단하면서도 어려웠다.

그 다음으로 급한 것은 제 2의 펠레 ‘호비뉴(Robinho)의 재능이었다.

카를로스와 반 니스텔루이는 계약기간이 내년까지였기에 아직 1년이라는 시간이 남아있었다.

다만 호비뉴는 머지않아 레알 마드리드를 떠나게 될지도 몰랐다.

호영이 알고 있는 미래대로라면 말이다.

하지만.

‘이번엔 몰라.’

현 레알 마드리드의 회장은 칼데론이 아닌 페레즈.

2008년 7월 말에 있을 회장 선거에서 별일만 일어나지 않는다면 페레즈가 3선에 성공할 듯 보였다.

그렇기에 그 무엇도 확신할 수 없었다.

호영은 그저 앞에 놓인 것만 착실하게 해내면 되는 것이었다.

‘난 내 할 것만 하면 돼.’

가장 먼저 이뤄내야 할 목표는 1군으로 승격하는 것.

그것뿐이었다.

5월 25일.

카스티야의 선수단이 카탈루냐 지방에 위치한 바르셀로나로 향했다.

리그폐막까지 단 한 경기 남은 제 41라운드.

리그 5위 카스티야와 리그 7위 바르셀로나B의 대결이 펼쳐질 예정이었다.

여기서 이기면 카스티야로서는 리그 5위를 확정 짓게 된다.

반면 바르셀로나B로서는 승리할 시 리그 6위로 뛰어오를 수 있는 절호의 기회였다.

그리고 그건 우호영도 마찬가지.

오늘 경기에 많은 것이 걸려있었다.

바르셀로나 유망주들의 재능은 둘째치더라도, 가장 중요한 호나우두의 재능이 걸려있었다.

‘반드시 이겨야 돼.’

공격수에게 있어서 가장 중요한 재능, ‘축구황제의 골 결정력(SU)’이었기에, 호영으로서도 결코 양보할 수 없는 승부였다.

그만큼 준비를 철저히 해왔기에 자신감이 있었다.

다만 문제가 되는 것이 하나 있다면 원정경기라는 점.

“젖내 나는 녀석!”

“너희 나라로 돌아가라!”

이러한 야유를 한 몸에 받으며 필드 위에 올라선 호영은 귀를 닫았다.

축구공만 뚫어져라 쳐다봤다.

승리 외에는 아무것도 생각하지 않았다.

할 수 있다.

그렇게 위닝 멘탈리티를 다지며 멘탈을 다스렸다.

경기가 시작된 것은 비가 한두 방울씩 떨어질 무렵이었다.

삐익-

킥오프.

선축을 가져간 바르셀로나는 저번과 마찬가지로 4-3-3 포메이션을 들고 나왔다.

시작부터 보얀(Bojan)을 중앙 스트라이커에 기용하여 1.5선에 배치하였다.

전술자체는 지난번과 같았는데, 거기에 다양한 세부전술이 추가되어 보다 더 날카롭고 견고하게 다듬어진 상태였다.

전에는 경험해보지 못한 축구.

그것이 카스티야의 선수들을 뒤흔들고 있었다.

점유율은 비등비등했지만, 실질적으로 위협적인 움직임을 보이는 것은 바르셀로나였다.

딱, 홈 관중들의 함성이 미친 듯이 터져 나오는 전반 10분까지는 말이다.

하지만 얼마 지나지 않아서였다.

관중석에 찬물이라도 끼얹은 듯 그 함성은 급격히 잦아들었다.

경기장에 울려 퍼지는 것은 빗소리와 해설진의 목소리뿐이었다.

[카스티야의 반격이 서서히 시작됩니다!]

[중원이 살아나면서 카스티야가 기세를 회복하고 있어요. 게다가 빗발이 거세지면서 경기 템포가 점차 빨라지고 있거든요? 바르셀로나는 이런 순간을 조심해야 합니다. 관중들도 몸소 느낄 정도로 위험한 상황이에요.]

[그렇습니다. 바르셀로나가 중앙의 구멍을 메우려다 보니 측면에 틈이 많이 생기고 있습니다. 지금이 가장 조심해야 할······.]

[아, 말씀하신 순간! 좌측 풀백으로 선발 출전한 마르코스가 과감한 오버래핑을 시도합니다!]

마르코스의 패스가 대지를 갈랐다.

타악!

공을 받은 것은 1-2선을 분주하게 오가던 호영이었다.

“올라가!”

카스티야가 중원에서의 점유율을 서서히 쌓아올리자, 그의 천재성이 빛을 발하기 시작했다.

바르셀로나로서는 처음 경험해보는 천재적인 재능.

지난 12월의 호영은 이미 2부 리거의 수준을 뛰어넘은 상태였다.

그로부터 얼마 지나지 않아서였다.

호영의 드리블이 순식간에 상대 수비수들을 모조리 벗겨내 버린 것은.

그 다음에는 골대가 한바탕 요동쳤다.

추적추적 떨어지는 빗물을 가르며, 골대 우측 상단에 정확히 꽂힌 호영의 중거리 슈팅이었다.

그것이 관중석의 반응을 극과 극으로 나뉘게 만들었다.

경악으로 물든 관중석의 홈팬들.

그들은 믿고 싶지 않은 현실에 차마 말을 잇지 못하고 있었다.

하지만 그와 반대로, 동쪽의 원정석은 열광의 도가니였다.

그 한 가운데, 적지 않게 흥분한 노인이 앉아있었다.

“흘흘.”

시간 내어 원정길에 참가한 페레즈 회장이었다.

그는 옆자리에 앉아있던 호르헤 발다노(Jorge Valdano) 단장에게 말하고 있었다.

“발보아는 방출대상에 올리게.”

페레즈와 발보아.

둘은 한때 각별한 사이였다.

페레즈가 회장직에 첫 부임했을 때부터 지금까지 9년간 봐온 사이였으니까.

특히 발보아가 올 시즌에 골을 넣었을 때만 해도 부활의 가능성이 살짝 보이기는 했다.

하지만 지금.

발보아를 굳이 데리고 있을 이유가 몽땅 사라졌다.

매주 2만 유로를 지급하면서까지 발보아를 데리고 있을 이유가 말이다.

당연했다.

프로세계에 정(情)이란 있다가도 없는 거니까.

필요 없는 이는 가차 없이 내보내고, 필요한 이를 그 자리에 채워 넣는 것이 프로스포츠이니까.

“그럼 빈자리는 누가 들어갑니까?”

“1순위는 우호영 외에 없어. 그게 곧 소시오들의 의견이야.”

명령이었다.

즉, 페레즈가 연임에 실패하지 않는 한 반드시 일어나게 될 일.

우호영의 승격이 99% 확정된 순간이었다.

“내년은 재밌어지겠구만.”

바르셀로나의 메시.

레알 마드리드의 우호영.

스페인 라 리가에 거대한 바람이 불어 닥치고 있었다.

In other words, it meant that Ho-young's initiation ceremony was going smoothly.

Ho-young gained a lot from the training that day.

Although it was impossible to gain much from the first day due to the short training time and limited interaction between players, he was about 50% successful in bringing the U-class talents of Carlos and Hovinho.

It laid the groundwork for coveting their S-class talents in the future.

And all of this in just one day.

"Good."

Everything was progressing sequentially.

Moreover, today's training was a great help in planning for the future.

"Let's start by scouting the most regrettable player to miss out on."

Zidane's ball trapping talent, which he planned to retire at the end of the season, was the most urgent.

The hidden condition was "Promotion to the first division," which was simple yet difficult.

Next in urgency was the talent of the second Pelé, Robinho.

Carlos and Van Nistelrooy had contracts until next year, so there was still one year left.

However, it was possible that Robinho would soon leave Real Madrid.

That is if things went as Ho-young knew from the future.

But.

"I don't know this time."

The current president of Real Madrid was not Calderón but Pérez.

If nothing unusual happened in the presidential election at the end of July 2008, Pérez seemed likely to succeed in his third term.

Therefore, nothing could be taken for granted.

Ho-young just had to faithfully do what was laid out in front of him.

"I just have to do my part."

The first goal to achieve was to be promoted to the first team.

That was all.

May 25th.

The Castilla squad headed to Barcelona, located in the Catalonia region.

The 41st round, with only one game left until the end of the league.

A match between 5th place Castilla and 7th place Barcelona B was scheduled.

A win here would secure Castilla's 5th place in the league.

On the other hand, for Barcelona B, a victory would be a golden opportunity to jump to 6th place in the league.

And the same was true for Ho-young.

A lot was at stake in today's game.

Aside from the talents of the Barcelona prospects, the most important talent of Ronaldo was at stake.

"I must win."

For a striker, the most important talent, "The Emperor of Football's Goal Scoring (SU)," was a match that Ho-young could not afford to lose.

He was confident because he had prepared thoroughly.

The only problem was that it was an away game.

"You smell like milk!"

"Go back to your country!"

Receiving such jeers, Ho-young took to the field with his ears closed.

He only stared at the soccer ball.

He thought of nothing but victory.

He could do it.

With that winning mentality, he controlled his mental state.

The game started when a few drops of rain began to fall.

Whistle-

Kickoff.

Barcelona, who had the first kick, came out with a 4-3-3 formation, just like last time.

From the start, Bojan was used as the central striker and placed in the 1.5 line.

The tactic itself was the same as last time, but with various detailed tactics added, it was sharpened and made more robust.

A type of soccer they had never experienced before.

That was shaking the Castilla players.

The possession was even, but Barcelona was the one showing more threatening movements.

That was until the 10th minute of the first half when the home crowd's cheers exploded like crazy.

But it wasn't long after.

As if cold water had been poured into the stands, the cheers quickly subsided.

The only sounds echoing in the stadium were the rain and the commentators' voices.

[Castilla's counterattack is slowly starting!]

[As the midfield comes to life, Castilla is regaining momentum. Plus, as the rain gets heavier, the pace of the game is speeding up, right? Barcelona needs to be careful at this moment. It's a dangerous situation that even the fans can feel.]

[That's right. As Barcelona tries to fill the gap in the center, there are many openings on the sides. Now is the most critical moment to be careful...]

[Ah, the moment you mentioned! Marcos, who started as the left fullback, attempts a bold overlapping!]

Marcos' pass split the ground.

Thwack!

The one who received the ball was Ho-young, who had been busily moving between the 1-2 lines.

"Move up!"

As Castilla slowly built up possession in the midfield, his genius began to shine.

A genius talent that Barcelona was experiencing for the first time.

By last December, Ho-young had already surpassed the level of a second division player.

It wasn't long before that happened.

Ho-young's dribble instantly stripped all the opposing defenders.

What followed was a tumult at the goalpost.

Cutting through the drizzling rain, Ho-young's mid-range shot was precisely lodged in the upper right corner of the goal.

That split the reaction in the stands to extremes.

The home fans in the stands were colored with astonishment.

They couldn't continue to speak in the face of a reality they didn't want to believe.

However, on the contrary, the eastern away section was a cauldron of ecstasy.

In the middle of it all, an excited old man was sitting.

"Heh heh."

It was President Pérez, who had taken the time to join the away trip.

He was speaking to Jorge Valdano, the director sitting next to him.

"Put Balboa on the transfer list."

Pérez and Balboa.

The two had a special relationship.

They had known each other for nine years since Pérez first took office as president.

Especially when Balboa scored a goal this season, there seemed to be a slight possibility of revival.

But now.

There was no reason to keep Balboa.

No reason to keep him while paying 20,000 euros a week.

It was natural.

In the professional world, affection can be fleeting.

It's professional sports to ruthlessly let go of those who are not needed and fill their place with those who are needed.

"Then who will fill the vacancy?"

"There's no one but Woo Ho-young as the first choice. That's the opinion of the socios."

It was an order.

In other words, as long as Pérez did not fail to be re-elected, it was something that was bound to happen.

The moment when Woo Ho-young's promotion was 99% confirmed.

"Next year will be interesting."

Barcelona's Messi.

Real Madrid's Woo Ho-young.

A giant wind was blowing in the Spanish La Liga.

지난 12월, 우호영에게 처참히 털렸던 기억이 아직도 선명한 부스케츠였다.

그것은 분명 악몽이었다.

그렇기에, 그런 상황을 겪지 않기 위해 혹독한 훈련을 치러왔다.

누누이 약점이라 평가받아온 대인방어 능력을 키웠다.

하지만 결과는 같았다.

자신이 비약적인 성장을 이뤄낸 사이, 우호영은 그보다 더 눈부신 성장을 이룩해낸 것이었다.

불과 5개월 만에 벌어진 일.

작년에 상대했던 우호영은 다가서지도 못할 벽이 되어버렸다.

그렇게, 부스케츠는 또 한 번 우호영이란 문턱에서 좌절감을 맛봐야했다.

그리고 이어지는 호영의 치고 달리기에, 중앙 수비수들까지 속수무책으로 당하고 말았다.

축구 참 쉽게하는 것 같았다.

하지만 실상은 전혀 그렇지 않았다.

우호영이 여기에 오기까지에는 동료들의 보이지 않는 도움이 있었다.

그걸 알고 있는 호영은 두 어깨가 무거웠다.

그 책임감을 해소할 수 있는 것은 오로지 결과.

득점뿐이었다.

그리고 바로 순간, 문전 앞에 선 호영의 눈매가 매섭게 구부러졌고.

조금도 지체하지 않았다.

슈팅 타이밍을 잡자마자 오른발을 간결하고 힘있게 뻗었다.

좌측구석으로 깔려 들어가는 땅볼 슈팅.

이미 수차례 기적 같은 선방을 보여줬던 골키퍼 핀토가 필사적으로 온몸을 날렸다.

허나, 이번에는 실점을 허용하고 말았다.

알고도 막을 수 없는 슈팅이었으니까.

철렁!

호영이 찬 슈팅이 골네트에 꽂히는 순간, 경기장의 모든 소리가 멎은 듯 고요해졌다.

관중석을 가득 채운 홈팬들의 응원소리가 일제히 멈췄다.

그러길 잠깐.

직후 꾸레(Cule)들의 탄식이 흘러나왔다.

반대로 그라운드 위는 뜨거운 함성으로 가득찼다.

“오와아아아아!”

“호우!”

후반 28분.

수비수·미드필더 할 것 없이 카스티야의 선수들이 일제히 달려와 호영을 잡아먹을 듯 덮쳤다.

그들 모두는 이 한 골을 자축할 자격이 있었다.

모두가 함께 만들어낸 골이었으니까.

그리고 이 쐐기골로, 바르셀로나는 지옥의 나락으로 떨어졌다.

경기종료 직전 보얀이 만회골을 넣는 데 성공했지만 거기까지였다.

기적은 일어나지 않았다.

삐익-

경기종료를 알리는 호각소리.

그 무렵 전광판에는 3-1이라는 스코어가 표기돼있었다.

지난 경기보다 더욱 처참한 결과가, 홈 관중들에게 치욕스러운 악몽을 선사했다.

그 시각 호영은 고개를 푹 숙인 채 바닥에 주저앉아있는 보얀을 바라보았다.

다른 팀이었다면 유니폼을 바꿔 입었겠지만 상대는 바르셀로나였다.

경기 일정상으로는 아무런 문제가 없었기 때문이다.

상식적으로, 한 차례의 평가전도 없이 올림픽 무대에 바로 오르는 것은 웬만해서 있을 수 없는 일이다.

아무리 호영의 실력이 검증되었다 하더라도 축구는 기본적으로 팀 게임이다.

그런 의미에서, 박성호 감독에게 있어 호영은 도박수나 마찬가지였다.

나이와 실력.

그런 것쯤은 상관없었다.

다만, 기강이 강한 올림픽대표팀 분위기에 적응할 수 있는지가 관건이었다.

그날 저녁.

“크흠.”

-허허, 그래. 감독님이야.

포항 스틸러스, 한국 U20대표팀 감독, 한국 성인대표팀 코치.

라울을 그림자처럼 쫓아다니며 1.5선에서 활개치고 다니는 우호영 때문이었다.

좌측 수비를 맡고 있는 조쉬 가드더(Josh Gardner)는 심지어 이런 생각이 들기까지 했다.

‘지단······?’

2선에 있어야할 지단이 마치 1선으로 올라온 듯한 느낌이었다.

바로 지금.

중원에서 시작되는 지단의 빌드 업이, 1선까지 이어져 반 코트를 장악하고 있다는 것을 알 수 있었다.

더해, 중원에 우뚝 서 있는 구티가 95%에 달하는 패스성공률을 보이면서 레알의 경기장악력을 극대화하고 있었다.

중원부터 1선까지.

어디에서든 안정적인 흐름이 흐르고 있었고, 레알 마드리드는 그것을 놓치지 않았다.

[레알 마드리드, 전반전 이후에 사라졌던 활력이 점점 되살아나고 있습니다. 역시 젊음은 다르다 이건가요?]

[하하. 아무래도 그런 것 같군요. 우호영 선수, 중원에서 배급되는 볼을 잘 처리하고 있어요. 덕분에 2선과 중원 사이의 공백이 점점 메워지고 있습니다. 텅 비어있던 중간지점에 징검다리를 놓은 것처럼 말이죠.]

[우호영 선수가 주어진 임무를 잘 수행하고 있다는 뜻이군요?]

[포지션의 이해도가 높다고 할 수 있겠죠. 교체 아웃되었던 사비올라가 막강한 공격력을 지닌 선수였다면, 우호영은 조화를 이룰 수 있는 능력을 두루 겸비했다고 볼 수 있습니다. 여기서 과연 어떤 플레이를 보여줄지 기대가 되는군요. 지반은 다 쌓았어요.]

그 말이 떨어지기 무섭게, 레알 마드리드가 공격카드를 꺼내들었다.

우측의 호비뉴(Robinho)를 기점으로 한 공격전개였다.

화려한 개인기가 주특기인 그는, 헛다리짚기로 선수 하나를 벗겨내자마자 측면돌파를 시도했다.

중앙에서는 호영의 위협적인 공간침투가 이어지고 있었다.

그 모습이 호비뉴의 눈에 들어왔다.

그러길 잠깐.

호비뉴는 짐짓 고민하는 모습을 내비치더니 시선을 다른 쪽으로 돌렸다.

호영에게 다이렉트로 패스하면 좋은 기회가 나올법한 상황이었지만, 호비뉴의 선택은 달랐다.

타악.

페널티 박스 바깥쪽, 지단에게 깔아주는 패스였다.

그리고 그 순간이었다.

슥.

지단의 미간이 팍 좁혀들더니 눈빛이 매섭게 변했다.

강렬한 햇볕이 내리쬐던 경기장이 한순간에 얼어붙은 것 같았다.

극도의 긴장상태.

그 가운데, 지단의 발이 움직였다.

툭.

“···!!”

수비 뒷공간을 한 번에 무너트리는 로빙스루패스.

그 틈을 파고든 이는 다름 아닌 우호영이었다.

타악!

경악스러운 스피드로 오프사이드라인을 붕괴시킨 호영의 눈이 전방으로 향했다.

남은 것은 골키퍼 하나.

6만 7천여 관중의 시선이 그 한 곳에 몰려들었다.

“오오오오오!”

“Shooot! Kiiiiid!!”

시애틀의 팬들이라기보다는 축구를 보러 온 시애틀의 시민들이 압도적으로 많았기에, 그들로서는 골이 터져 나오면 마냥 좋을 뿐이었다.

그 짧은 순간, 관중들은 고래고래 소리 지르며 호영을 응원하기 시작했다.

관중들의 함성이 경기장을 뒤흔들었다.

하지만 페널티 박스 안은 여전히 고요했다.

우호영.

속도를 줄이면서 숨을 한 번 토해냈다.

“후아.”

긴장감을 떨쳐냈다.

그저, 평상시와 다를 것 없다고 생각하면서 공 하나에 온 정신을 기울였다.

그리고 침착하게.

지단이 살짝 띄어준 로빙 패스를 가슴으로 받아냈다.

그 순간, 부랴부랴 뛰쳐나오는 골키퍼의 모습이 눈에 비쳤다.

호영의 한쪽 입꼬리가 부드럽게 휘어졌다.

117. The Monster Reaching Out to the World (2)

When Ho-young took control of the ball, the local Seattle commentators expressed their surprise.

[Woo Ho-young has entered the game. In fact, he's not a well-known player to us, but looking at his career... he's leaving footsteps that no one else can imitate.]

[He holds over six titles for being the youngest in the World Cup and the Spanish second division. Locally, he's considered the future of Real Madrid.]

[Wow, we might see an Asian wave hit the world of soccer.]

It was a sight that would amaze any soccer enthusiast.

An Asian, traditionally undervalued in soccer, and a boy not even in high school yet, was playing for Real Madrid, which was nothing short of sensational.

As people's attention was drawn to him, Ho-young calmly rolled the ball and widened his view.

'They're not an opponent to take lightly.'

Founded in 1974, this team had been reborn last year as Seattle Sounders FC, and since then, they had made significant financial investments to recruit excellent players, resulting in a quite solid squad.

The talents of the players Ho-young had checked confirmed this.

'There's no A-grade, but there's quite a lot of B-grade.'

If compared, it was a higher level than Castilla.

The current Real defense, composed of 1.5 teams, was not an easy match for them.

'This could be interesting.'

For some reason, he felt a sense of freshness.

Especially since there were as many as 60,000 spectators.

Playing soccer at the Di Stefano Stadium, which only held 6,000 people, and now playing here, it felt as if his own presence had grown along with the crowd.

But more than that, he was eager to show off his play.

So he moved.

Thwack, thwack.

He returned the pass to Zidane, closing the gap.

Then Zidane's pass came back, and Ho-young instinctively sent a ground pass to Raúl on the left.

At first, there was a slight discord.

But after about five minutes of syncing up, his thoughts quickly changed.

'It's comfortable.'

It was a curious thing.

He had plenty of experience syncing up with Zidane, but not so much with Raúl, Guti, or Robinho.

Yet it felt comfortable.

It was almost like playing at Castilla.

The seniors' consideration and Zidane's build-up play helped Ho-young become one with the team.

This was what being world-class was all about.

Moreover, Ho-young showed incredible adaptability, quickly elevating his game performance.

Gradually increasing his activity, he chased after Raúl as if he owned the place, expanding his range of action.

Shadow striker.

He faithfully performed the role of a second striker, showcasing his playmaking skills.

Meanwhile, Real Madrid increased their possession to 72%, dominating the game one-sidedly.

The ball had been in Seattle's half for over 10 minutes.

"Fuck."

As the game wasn't going well, the Seattle players began to curse.

It was manageable until Saviola was substituted out.

But after Woo Ho-young entered, the feeling changed significantly.

Before, it was Zidane and Guti in the midfield who were threatening, but now it was Raúl's movement that made it difficult to even breathe.

It was because of Woo Ho-young, who was chasing Raúl like a shadow and flying around the 1.5 line.

Josh Gardner, who was in charge of the left defense, even thought this.

'Zidane...?'

It felt as if Zidane, who should have been in the second line, had moved up to the front line.

Right now.

Zidane's build-up starting from the midfield was connecting up to the front line, dominating half the court.

Additionally, Guti, standing tall in the midfield, was maximizing Real's control of the game with a pass success rate of 95%.

From the midfield to the front line.

There was a stable flow everywhere, and Real Madrid did not miss it.

[Real Madrid is gradually reviving the vitality that disappeared after the first half. Is this the difference youth makes?]

[Haha. It seems so. Woo Ho-young is handling the ball well distributed from the midfield. Thanks to him, the gap between the second line and the midfield is being filled. It's like he's laid a stepping stone in the empty middle area.]

[Does that mean Woo Ho-young is performing his given task well?]

[You could say he has a high understanding of his position. If Saviola, who was substituted out, was a player with powerful offensive capabilities, Woo Ho-young has the ability to create harmony. I'm looking forward to seeing what kind of play he will show us. The groundwork is all set.]

As soon as those words were spoken, Real Madrid pulled out their offensive card.

It was an attack initiated by Robinho on the right.

Known for his flashy individual skills, he attempted a flank breakthrough as soon as he shook off a player with a feint.

In the center, Ho-young's threatening space penetration continued.

Robinho noticed him.

Just for a moment.

Robinho pretended to ponder and then turned his gaze elsewhere.

It was a situation where a direct pass to Ho-young could have created a good opportunity, but Robinho chose differently.

Thwack.

He passed the ball to Zidane outside the penalty box.

And that was the moment.

Swoosh.

Zidane's brows narrowed, and his eyes turned fierce.

The stadium, bathed in intense sunlight, seemed to freeze in an instant.

A state of extreme tension.

Amidst that, Zidane's foot moved.

Thwack.

"...!!"

A lobbed through pass that shattered the defense's backspace in one go.

The one who broke through that gap was none other than Woo Ho-young.

Thwack!

With astonishing speed, Ho-young collapsed the offside line, and his eyes focused ahead.

Only the goalkeeper remained.

The gaze of 67,000 spectators converged on that one spot.

"Ooooooh!"

"Shooot! Kiiiiid!!"

More than the fans of Seattle, there were overwhelmingly more citizens who had come to watch soccer, and for them, it was simply delightful to see a goal scored.

In that brief moment, the crowd began to cheer loudly for Ho-young.

The cheers of the spectators shook the stadium.

But inside the penalty box, it was still quiet.

Woo Ho-young.

He slowed down and exhaled once.

"Huah."

He shook off the tension.

He just focused all his attention on the ball, as if it were no different from usual.

And calmly.

He chested down the lobbed pass that Zidane had gently lifted.

At that moment, he saw the goalkeeper rushing out in a hurry.

The corner of Ho-young's mouth curved up softly.

오전에는 투어 주최 측인 아디다스의 팬 사인회 일정이 있었고, 오후에는 리글리 필드에 도착하여 시카고 컵스와 휴스턴의 MLB 경기를 관람하였다.

특히 사제지간답게, 피자를 만드는 내내 우호영을 챙기는 지단의 모습이 가히 일품이었다(라고, 쓰라며 지단이 피자를 주면서 협박하였다).

참고로 지단이 피자를 너무 못 만드는 바람에, 우호영이 직접 그를 도와 그럭저럭 괜찮은 피자를 만들 수 있었다.

발기술이 좋다고 손기술까지 좋은 것은 아닌 듯하다.

*위 사진은, 쭉 늘어진 치즈를 지단의 목에 걸치며 “치즈목걸이에요!”라고 우호영이 장난을 치는 장면이다.]

117. The Monster Reaching Out to the World (2)

In the morning, there was a fan signing event organized by Adidas, the tour sponsor, and in the afternoon, they arrived at Wrigley Field to watch an MLB game between the Chicago Cubs and Houston.

The Cubs' owner, the Chicago Tribune executives, welcomed them, allowing them to watch the game in the best environment.

After the game, Raúl González exchanged jerseys with starting pitcher Carlos Marmol, strengthening the clubs' friendship.

The next day was given as free time for the players.

Zinedine Zidane, acting as Ho-young's guardian, visited Gino's East, a famous pizza restaurant in Chicago.

Real Madrid's official photographer, Simon, followed them like a paparazzi, taking photos for the fans, and the pictures taken that day became a hot topic on Real Madrid's official website.

The content of the column with the attached photo was as follows:

[Zidane and Woo Ho-young Take on Traditional Chicago-Style Pizza Making!

The famous teacher-student duo of Los Blancos took on a pizza-making event at Chicago's famous 'Gino's East Pizza Restaurant.'

They made a traditional Chicago-style deep-dish pizza, and managed to bake a fairly appetizing pizza, earning praise from the restaurant's owner.

Especially fitting for a teacher-student relationship, Zidane's care for Woo Ho-young while making the pizza was truly exceptional (as he threatened while handing over the pizza).

By the way, because Zidane was so bad at making pizza, Woo Ho-young had to help him out, and they managed to make a decent pizza.

It seems that good foot skills don't necessarily translate to good hand skills.

*The photo above shows a scene where Woo Ho-young playfully puts stretched cheese around Zidane's neck, saying, "It's a cheese necklace!"]

The column was scrapped into the Korean Ho-woo-ho-woo fan cafe and soon spread to various online community sites.

Netizens' reactions were explosive.

[ㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋㅋ This combo is crazy]

└Playing around with Zidane, wow... that's no jokeㅠ

└Ho-young has such a mischievous sideㅋ I thought he was just stoic from the interviews.

└Ho0 Forever♥♥♥

└Wish he'd come to Korea soon. I bought soccer tickets for the first timeㅋㅋ Is Zidane coming too?

└It's a riot, you can tell it's staged just by looking at it ㅋㅋㅋㅋ

└Keyboard warriors, come on in~

The response was explosive.

But that was just the tip of the iceberg.

On the 9th at 1 PM, ahead of the game against Chicago Fire SC, the announcement of Real Madrid's starting lineup caused quite a stir.

Ho-young's name was listed in the starting lineup.

This sparked numerous discussions about Ho-young's current standing within the team.

[Feels a bit off. Are they planning to use him just as a backup resource next season? Then maybe...]

[Maybe freeze to death. It's preseason, they're probably just giving the prospects a chance. What's the fuss?]

Both were valid points.

In fact, Schuster had both thoughts in his mind.

In the visiting team's coach's office at Soldier Field, home of the Chicago Bears football team, Schuster was still deliberating over the detailed tactics.

'Ho-young and Robinho are hard to coexist.'

Then there was only one answer.

Schuster checked the starting lineup again, lost in thought.

It was as good as a death list.

The one most surprised by this was Ho-young.

Having confirmed it himself in the players' locker room, he couldn't help but doubt his eyes as he looked over the list again and again.

His eyes rolled busily, and his Adam's apple bobbed up and down.

Goalkeeper - Codina (26)

Defenders - Roberto Carlos (35), Pepe (25), Sergio Díaz (23), Miguel Torres (22)

Midfielders - Mahamadou Diarra (27), Tebar (22), Callejón (21), Woo Ho-young (14), Zinedine Zidane (36·4th captain)

Forward - Robinho (24)

'Attacking midfielder...'

The attacking midfielder in the 4-2-3-1 formation, responsible for leading the offense.

Last season, it was a position typically held by Guti or Zidane, tactically the most important spot.

But to start him in such a position meant that they were considering him not just as a stopgap but as a key member.

In other words, whether he would rise as the protagonist on the main stage or just as an ordinary role would be decided in today's game.

However, there was one point of concern.

'Robinho as the lone striker?'

It was definitely strange to place Robinho, who had strong inside forward tendencies, as the lone striker.

'Am I supposed to be the main focus, or am I supposed to support Robinho?'

Schuster had yet to say anything about that.

'He hasn't said a word.'

There was only one thing for certain.

'I'll give it my all.'

To some, it might just be an informal match to take lightly, but not for Ho-young.

His future for the next season was at stake.

That's why he had to do well.

He had yet to show the coach many things.

Like the Emperor of Football's goal-scoring ability, for instance.

He was eager to show it, his heels twitching.

And shortly after.

As he passed through the unusually short players' tunnel and onto the ground, the cheers of some 60,000 spectators erupted.

Among them, there was someone who glanced at Ho-young.

Not the players of Chicago Fire SC, but Robinho's gaze.

It was a look that said he was going to take Ho-young down in today's game.

'It's been a while since I've felt this.'

The day he had a training match with the same team as the soccer emperor Ronaldo.

The feeling was exactly the same as now.

That feeling served as kindling to ignite Ho-young's competitive spirit.

He understood why Coach Schuster had given no instructions.

At the same time, he realized.

The protagonist of today would be the one who took control of the game.

And their confrontation began at 2:15 PM.

It was also then that Ho-young began to take the lead.

컵스의 구단주 시카고 트리뷴(Chicago Tribune) 임원들의 환대를 받아 최고의 환경에서 경기를 관람할 수 있었다.

경기가 끝난 뒤에는, 라울 곤잘레스가 선발투수 카를로스 마몰(Carlos marmol)과 유니폼을 교환하며 구단 간의 친분을 다지기도 했다.

다음 날은 선수들에게 자유시간이 주어졌다.

지네딘 지단은 호영의 보호자를 자처하여 시카고의 명소 지노스 이스트(Gino's East) 피자 레스토랑에 방문하였다.

레알 마드리드의 공식 사진사 시몬(Simon)은 파파라치마냥 그들의 뒤를 쫓아 팬들을 위한 사진을 찍었는데, 그날 찍힌 사진이 레알 마드리드 공식 홈페이지에 올라가면서 연일 화제를 낳았다.

사진이 첨부된 칼럼의 내용은 이러했다.

[지단과 우호영, 정통 시카고식 피자 만들기에 도전하다!

사제지간으로 유명한 두 블랑코스는 시카고의 명소 ‘지노스 이스트 피자 레스토랑’에서 피자 만들기 이벤트에 도전하였다.

그들은 정통 시카고식 피자인 딥디쉬 피자(Deep dish pizza)를 만들었는데, 그럭저럭 먹음직스러운 피자를 구워내면서 레스토랑 사장의 칭찬을 받았다.

발기술이 좋다고 손기술까지 좋은 것은 아닌 듯하다.

위 칼럼은 한국의 호우호우형 팬 카페로 스크랩되었고, 그로부터 얼마 지나지 않아 각종 온라인 커뮤니티 사이트로 퍼지게 되었다.
